question_text: Which statement best compares fossil fuels and nuclear energy in terms of carbon dioxide emissions?

options.label: A
options.text: Fossil fuels produce no CO₂, but nuclear energy produces large amounts

options.label: B
options.text: Fossil fuels produce large amounts of CO₂ during combustion, while nuclear energy produces very little CO₂ during operation

options.label: C
options.text: Both fossil fuels and nuclear energy produce equal amounts of CO₂

options.label: D
options.text: Nuclear energy produces more CO₂ than fossil fuels because of radioactive waste

correct_answer: Fossil fuels produce large amounts of CO₂ during combustion, while nuclear energy produces very little CO₂ during operation
explanation: Burning fossil fuels releases stored carbon as CO₂, contributing to climate change. Nuclear energy generates electricity without burning fuel, so it produces negligible CO₂ during operation, though some is produced during construction and uranium mining.
